title: A guard flag does not prevent re-entrancy across await in single-threaded async code
rule: In cooperative single-threaded async runtimes (JS event loop, Python asyncio), control only leaves a function at a suspension point (`await`/`yield`), so a check-then-act guard that straddles the first await is still a race: every coroutine that runs up to the guard before any of them suspends passes it. Mutate the guard state — set the boolean, or store the in-flight Promise/Future for dedupe — SYNCHRONOUSLY before the first await, never after. There is no implicit critical section between awaits.
triggers: duplicate fetch fired even though I have an inFlight flag
debounce / once guard not working with async/await
two requests sent despite isLoading check
concurrent calls both pass the if (busy) return check
